pi_items.source: Task description and verifier expectations: dashboard service initially created, proxy service to be added by user; export_result.sh validates both services (lines 14, 39)
Multiple script references: setup_task.sh line 53 (default Docker API port), export_result.sh lines 62, 69, 78 (http://proxy:2375/*); standard Docker Engine API port

Docker Engine API documentation verified via web search and Docker API reference; /containers/json is tested in export_result.sh line 62 expecting 200 OK; /containers/{id}/stats is the Docker API endpoint for container statistics with 12345 as a concrete container ID example

All three are real Docker Engine API endpoints verified via web search and Docker API documentation. /containers/create (POST) tested in export_result.sh line 69-70 expecting 403/401/405. /secrets tested in export_result.sh line 76-78 expecting 403/401. /images/json is a valid endpoint but not explicitly tested in the verifier script.
verifier.py line 123: passed = score >= 70; also documented in verifier.py line 13 'Pass Threshold: 70 points'

privileged_info_summary: The docker_socket_proxy task is a security hardening exercise that requires securing a vulnerable Docker monitoring stack. The project directory is /home/ga/projects/monitor-stack. The task requires two services: a dashboard service (initially running with raw Docker socket access) and a proxy service (to be created) running on port 2375. The allowed endpoints are GET /containers/json and GET /containers/{id}/stats (exemplified as /containers/12345/stats). Blocked endpoints include POST /containers/create, GET /secrets, and /images/json. The task passes with a score of 70 or higher. The data is synthetically generated (Docker containers and Python application created by setup scripts), not sourced from external datasets.
